idea.hook: Think you know your cosmic neighborhood? Think again!
facts: Our entire Solar System, including the Oort Cloud, spans approximately 2 light-years.
Our Solar System resides within the Local Bubble, a vast cavity of hot gas roughly 300 light-years across.
idea.payoff: Your *entire* Solar System is a microscopic speck within our immediate 'local' cosmic environment!
idea.title: Your "Neighborhood" is MASSIVE! 🤯🌌

scenes.visual: Deep space background with subtle distant stars. Text appears over it.
A vibrant, rotating Earth. As text appears, a small label points to Earth showing its diameter.
Earth shrinks dramatically to become a tiny dot within a wider view of the entire Solar System (sun, planets, and faint Oort Cloud boundary). A measurement line spans the full 2 light-years.

The Solar System (from previous scene) becomes a microscopic speck in the center of a much larger, transparent, glowing spherical 'bubble' (the Local Bubble). A measurement line spans the 300 Light-Years.

scenes.text: Your Solar System: A MICROSCOPIC SPECK!
scenes.visual: The view zooms out rapidly, making the entire Local Bubble itself a tiny, barely visible sphere against an immense, dark cosmic canvas of countless galaxies. The Solar System within is no longer visible.
